name: Streaming Replication Setup
description: Configure PostgreSQL streaming replication with a primary and standby server for high availability and read scaling.

steps.title: Create a replication role on the primary
steps.description: Create a dedicated user with replication privileges on the primary server.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -c "CREATE ROLE replicator WITH REPLICATION LOGIN PASSWORD 'repl_secure_pass';"

steps.title: Configure primary for replication
steps.description: Set the required WAL and replication parameters on the primary.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET wal_level = 'replica'; ALTER SYSTEM SET max_wal_senders = 5; ALTER SYSTEM SET wal_keep_size = '1GB';"
steps.tip: These changes require a PostgreSQL restart to take effect.

steps.title: Add replication entry to pg_hba.conf
steps.description: Allow the standby server to connect for replication.
steps.command: echo 'host replication replicator 10.0.0.0/24 scram-sha-256' | sudo tee -a /etc/postgresql/16/main/pg_hba.conf
steps.warning: Replace 10.0.0.0/24 with the actual IP range of your standby server. Restart PostgreSQL after this change.

steps.title: Create a base backup on the standby
steps.description: Clone the primary database to the standby server using pg_basebackup.
steps.tip: The -R flag creates standby.signal and sets primary_conninfo in postgresql.auto.conf automatically.

steps.title: Start the standby and verify replication
steps.description: Start PostgreSQL on the standby and confirm it is receiving WAL from the primary.
steps.command: sudo systemctl start postgresql && psql -U postgres -c "SELECT pg_is_in_recovery();"

steps.title: Check replication status on the primary
steps.description: Monitor replication lag and standby connection status from the primary.
steps.command: psql -U postgres -c "SELECT client_addr, state, sent_lsn, write_lsn, flush_lsn, replay_lsn, replay_lag FROM pg_stat_replication;"

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# PostgreSQL Streaming Replication Status Check
set -euo pipefail

USER="postgres"

echo "=== Server Role ==="
IS_STANDBY=$(psql -U "$USER" -tAc "SELECT pg_is_in_recovery();")
if [ "$IS_STANDBY" = "t" ]; then
  echo "This server is a STANDBY (replica)"
  echo ""
  echo "=== Replication status (standby) ==="
  psql -U "$USER" -c "
  SELECT pg_last_wal_receive_lsn() AS received_lsn,
         pg_last_wal_replay_lsn() AS replayed_lsn,
         pg_last_xact_replay_timestamp() AS last_replay_time,
         now() - pg_last_xact_replay_timestamp() AS replay_lag;
  "
else
  echo "This server is the PRIMARY"
  echo ""
  echo "=== Connected standbys ==="
  psql -U "$USER" -c "
  SELECT client_addr,
         state,
         sent_lsn,
         write_lsn,
         flush_lsn,
         replay_lsn,
         replay_lag
  FROM pg_stat_replication;
  "
  echo ""
  echo "=== WAL settings ==="
  psql -U "$USER" -c "
  SELECT name, setting
  FROM pg_settings
  WHERE name IN ('wal_level', 'max_wal_senders', 'wal_keep_size', 'synchronous_commit');
  "
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Replication slots ==="
psql -U "$USER" -c "
SELECT slot_name, slot_type, active, restart_lsn
FROM pg_replication_slots;
"

faq.question: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous replication?
faq.answer: Asynchronous replication (default) lets the primary commit without waiting for the standby. Synchronous replication waits for the standby to confirm the write, guaranteeing zero data loss but adding latency.

faq.question: How do I promote a standby to primary?
faq.answer: Run 'pg_ctl promote -D /var/lib/postgresql/16/main' or 'SELECT pg_promote();' in psql. The standby exits recovery mode and becomes a read-write primary. This is irreversible.

faq.question: Can I read from a standby?
faq.answer: Yes, standbys accept read-only queries by default (hot_standby = on). This is commonly used to offload reporting queries and read-heavy workloads from the primary.
